_metadata.title: Canadian Cosmetic Ingredient Database - Research Data
sources: EU Regulation 1223/2009 Annex III - 26 Fragrance Allergens
Personal Care Products Council INCI Dictionary
_metadata.notes: The Health Canada Hotlist contains ~500 entries. The canada.ca site blocks automated scraping. The prohibited/restricted entries below are compiled from multiple secondary regulatory sources and may not be 100% complete. Always verify against the official Hotlist at canada.ca for regulatory compliance.

section1_health_canada_hotlist.description: Health Canada Cosmetic Ingredient Hotlist - Prohibited and Restricted Ingredients

prohibited_ingredients.reason: Neurotoxin, bioaccumulation. Part of Health Canada mercury reduction strategy.
Toxic heavy metal. Intentional addition prohibited (trace contaminants addressed separately)
Toxic (as an intentional ingredient; permitted as denaturant trace in ethanol)
Presence on Prescription Drug List; sole therapeutic functions

prohibited_ingredients.notes: Prohibited as direct dye; restricted when used with oxidizer in hair dye
Prohibited in cosmetics other than anti-dandruff shampoos (where restricted)

restricted_ingredients.conditions: Minimum pH of 3.5 when used as exfoliating agents. Must include sunburn alert cautionary statement.
restricted_ingredients.product_types: All cosmetics when used as exfoliants

restricted_ingredients.conditions: Minimum pH of 3.5 when used as exfoliating agents
Not for use in products for children under 3 years

max_concentration_details.conditions: Must carry cautionary labels and directions for safe use
Products undergoing forced convection (blow-drying) or heating (flat-ironing)

restricted_ingredients.conditions: Added to CEPA 1999 Schedule 1. Combined limit applies when both p-hydroxyanisole and hydroquinone used in nail products.
Permitted in fragrances and lotions

max_concentration_details.product_type: Rinse-off hair care, conditioning agent (chain lengths >=16 C)

restricted_ingredients.conditions: Rinse-off products only
Warning not required when used as pH adjuster at <=0.1%. Not for use in products for children under 3 years.
Permitted only in anti-dandruff/anti-seborrheic shampoos and scalp treatments

restricted_ingredients.max_concentration: 0.254% as fluoride ion (1500 ppm in dentifrices)
restricted_ingredients.conditions: Permitted only in dentifrices/oral care products
Prohibited in leave-on cosmetics. Permitted only in rinse-off products.
Prohibited in leave-on cosmetics. Permitted only in rinse-off products.
Hair dye use only. Must include warnings about skin sensitization.
Hair dyes and rinse-off hair lotions/shampoos only
Anti-dandruff shampoos only
Mouthwash only
Depilatory products only. Not permitted in aerosol products.
Hair removal (depilatory) products only
Revised restrictions re: chronic exposure concerns. Must be asbestos-free.
Not for use in mouthwash/oral products. Not for products for children under 12.
Cosmetics intended to be diluted in bath water may exceed 10%
Restricted due to therapeutic properties and potential for skin irritation
Must not exceed certain concentrations; not recommended for lip products or products likely to be ingested
Permitted as preservatives within limits
Not for use in leave-on products for diaper area of children under 3
As preservative
Not allowed in products intended for mucous membranes
In cosmetic products other than nail products
In finished products containing bergamot or other furocoumarin-containing essential oils
Nail products only
Restricted use; concerns re: reproductive toxicity
Cosmetic use only (higher concentrations = drug)
Anti-dandruff products only. Not for use in leave-on products except as anti-dandruff agents.
Required as a denaturant in certain alcohol-containing products

proposed_changes_2026.details: Prohibition in leave-on hair products; limited concentrations in hair dyes and rinse-off
Prohibited in spray/aerosol products; max concentration limits for other topical products. Inhalation and sensitization concerns.
Moving from restricted to prohibited. High acute toxicity, neurotoxic, significant dermal absorption.
Removing exception for Symphytum officinale due to detection of hepatotoxic alkaloid echimidine

section2_eu_26_fragrance_allergens.description: EU 26 Fragrance Allergens requiring individual label disclosure per EU Regulation 1223/2009 Annex III
section2_eu_26_fragrance_allergens.note_canada: Canada has moved toward requiring disclosure of fragrance allergens on labels, aligning with EU standards. Manufacturers should individually list these allergens in INCI ingredient lists when above threshold concentrations.
section2_eu_26_fragrance_allergens.note_eu_expansion: EU Regulation 2023/1545 expands the list from 26 to 80+ allergens. New EU labelling deadlines: July 31, 2026 (new products) and July 31, 2028 (existing products).

allergens.found_in: Many essential oils, lily of the valley
Lily of the valley type fragrances
Lily of the valley type fragrances
allergens.note: BANNED in EU since 2021 - no longer permitted

section3_ingredient_functions.description: Standard cosmetic ingredient functions/roles used in INCI and cosmetic formulation

functions.description: Softens and smooths skin by preventing water loss and filling gaps between skin cells
Attracts and retains moisture from the environment and deeper skin layers
Reduces surface tension to aid cleansing, foaming, wetting, and emulsifying
Prevents microbial growth (bacteria, yeast, mold) to extend product shelf life
Prevents oxidation of product ingredients and/or protects skin from free radical damage
Stabilizes mixtures of oil and water phases to form stable emulsions
Increases product viscosity and modifies flow properties (rheology)
Imparts color to the product or provides coverage on skin/hair
Absorbs, reflects, or scatters UV radiation to protect skin from sun damage
Imparts scent to the product or masks unpleasant odors of raw materials
Adjusts or maintains the pH of the product at a desired level
Forms a continuous film on skin, hair, or nails upon application
Improves the feel, appearance, and manageability of skin or hair
Dissolves other ingredients; the continuous phase in which other ingredients are dispersed
Binds metal ions to prevent them from affecting product stability or performance
Promotes removal of dead skin cells (chemical or physical)
Makes a transparent or translucent product opaque or pearly
Creates a protective barrier on skin surface to shield from irritants or moisture loss
Physically scrubs or polishes surfaces (skin, teeth)
Makes alcohol or other ingredients unpalatable to prevent ingestion
Increases flexibility and workability of film-forming polymers
Provides force to expel product from aerosol containers
Contracts or tightens body tissues, often for pore-minimizing effect
Adds volume and body to a product without contributing active function
Kills or inhibits growth of microorganisms on the skin

suppliers.name: Saffire Blue Inc.
suppliers.notes: Headquarters listed as Courtland, ON on some sources. Previously associated with Barrie, ON but current address is Woodstock.

suppliers.name: Windy Point Soap Making Supplies Inc.
suppliers.notes: Based in Calgary, AB (not Edmonton as originally noted)

suppliers.name: Canwax Inc.
suppliers.notes: Canada's largest wick supplier. Also carries soap making and personal care supplies. No showroom; pickup by advance order only.
specialties: Cosmetic packaging (amber glass, lip balm tubes)

section5_common_cosmetic_ingredients.description: 200+ common cosmetic ingredients organized by category with INCI names, CAS numbers, functions, typical usage rates, and Health Canada hotlist status

preservatives.hotlist_note: Max 0.14% individually. Not for leave-on products for diaper area of children under 3.
Max 0.14% individually. Not for leave-on products for diaper area of children under 3.

preservatives.note: EU fragrance allergen - must declare above threshold

preservatives.hotlist_note: Formaldehyde releaser - subject to formaldehyde limits
Formaldehyde releaser - subject to formaldehyde limits
Formaldehyde releaser - subject to formaldehyde limits
Max 0.0015% (15 ppm). Rinse-off only. PROHIBITED in leave-on.

colorants.note: Also used as physical sunscreen active

uv_filters_sunscreen_actives.note: Physical/mineral UV filter. Broad spectrum UVA+UVB.
Physical/mineral UV filter. Broad spectrum UVA+UVB.
UVB filter. Restricted in some jurisdictions (Hawaii).

fragrances_and_essential_oils.note: Must individually list EU 26 allergens if above thresholds
Contains limonene and citral (EU allergens). Furocoumarins restricted to 1 ppm in finished product.
Contains cinnamal and eugenol (EU allergens). Skin sensitizer at higher concentrations.

fragrances_and_essential_oils.hotlist_note: Must be FCF (furocoumarin-free). Furocoumarins restricted to 1 ppm in finished product.

active_and_functional_ingredients.hotlist_note: Concentration limits apply. Not recommended for lip products.
Max 2%. Not for children under 3 years.
Max 10% as exfoliant, min pH 3.5. Sunburn alert required.

ph_adjusters.note: Also an AHA at higher concentrations - subject to AHA restrictions as exfoliant
Used in small amounts for pH adjustment; larger amounts for soap making (saponification)

ph_adjusters.hotlist_note: As exfoliant: max 10%, min pH 3.5. No restriction as pH adjuster at low levels.

film_formers_and_polymers.note: Primary film former in nail polish

oral_care_specifics.hotlist_note: Max 0.254% as fluoride ion (approx 1500 ppm F) in dentifrices

makeup_specifics.hotlist_note: Must be asbestos-free. Revised restrictions re: chronic exposure.
